title: Chapter 358
content:
Chapter 358: Chapter 358 : The God Who Walks Among Mortals

Albert sweated heavily but he instantly launched a furious counter-offensive with a remarkably stern voice. "Are you seriously interrogating me about fabric stains right now? Can your eyes not see her current physical condition? Young miss literally spent two entire hours inside that dimension fighting, or rather beating the professor into total submission. Anyone with half a functioning brain cell can instantly deduce that it is merely intense physical sweat. Or the natural byproduct of a water element spell being used during their fight. Or perhaps that dimension simply had some kind of lake terrain."

Rora let out a painfully fake laugh. "Hey, I am fully aware of all that! And I am definitely not an idiot! But a highly entertaining possibility just flashed through my brain and completely hijacked my thoughts."

Her eyes sparkled with cruel hope. "I was honestly praying that the brat literally pissed herself in the middle of putting on the tough act. But if that was truly the case, she obviously would not be standing here right now flashing such a massive victorious smile."

She sighed with genuine disappointment. "It honestly would have been a delight to watch that arrogant brat look embarrassed as hell for once in her miserable life." Then her eyes sharpened. "Oh, but wait a minute. How exactly are you going to explain the inside-out pajamas?"

Albert panicked and said whatever his mind could form in the fraction of a second. "That is entirely your fault for never stepping outside your room and touching some grass once in a while! Wearing clothes inside out is literally the latest fashion trend sweeping through the main city right now."

He doubled down without a breath. "From noble teenagers to commoners, everyone is doing it. They call it the reversed aesthetic or something equally ridiculous. Some say it signifies rejecting societal expectations. Others simply treat it as a brilliant move to utilize both sides of the garment, so you can literally squeeze double the value out of the money you spent on it."

Rora raised an eyebrow. "The world sure has gone completely stupid. But still, I am positive she was wearing them correctly earlier tonight. I was mocking her inside my head over those stupid polar bears printed all over them."

Albert clenched his fist. "You are clearly hallucinating."

He saw her mouth opening to protest and immediately spoke over her. "And frankly, I refuse to entertain this conversation any further because you have completely lost every last shred of my respect tonight."

His voice turned genuinely disgusted. "You are a fully grown woman intensely scrutinizing the lower body of a teenage girl, analyzing mysterious stains, and interrogating a man about the positioning of her undergarments. That is not curiosity, Rora. That is textbook perversion, so sort yourself out immediately, because I refuse to be associated with this degeneracy any longer."

Albert yanked off his shoes, held them firmly in his hand to avoid making any sound, and started walking away barefoot. "I am leaving now. And I will be reporting your disgusting behavior directly to Lord Aleris."

Rora frantically scrambled after him while waving her hands in total desperation. "Hold on, hold on, hold on! Do not you dare do that to me! I swear I will fulfill any demand you make of me! So let us just bury this entire night and pretend we never even left the estate! You can totally pull that off, right? After all, we are trusted comrades who are supposed to look out for each other!"

Albert had a victorious grin plastered across his face as he watched her pleading expression. ’Got you, you complete idiot. I successfully locked down her dangerous mouth to protect myself and simultaneously walked away with a priceless blank check from this monster of a woman. And the best part is that she genuinely believes we just struck a mutually beneficial agreement, when the glorious truth is that I am winning completely on both sides.’

He let out a deeply satisfied sigh. ’Sometimes I genuinely feel like I am God himself walking among these pathetic mortals. Because my genius is simply operating on an entirely different level.’

The two of them retreated quietly as no further drama remained. The couple was heading back toward the Aleris estate, and there was nothing left to spy on.

But they were not the only audience tonight.

Delphine had watched the entire spectacle while remaining completely petrified behind her tree. The romance between her professor and the crazy senior had looked like absolutely nothing compared to what she had just witnessed.

First came the fear in the eyes of two strongest people whose enemies trembled at the mere mention of their names. Not just fear and shock, but the kind of actions and expressions that instantly reduced those legendary figures into the category of clueless children.

And then came the part that turned Delphine’s face a burning shade of crimson. She had not seen what happened inside the domain. But her mind had zero trouble filling in the blanks.

Her imagination took the dirtiest turn available and constructed an elaborate scene of the most violent bondage situation her brain could manufacture. And throughout that entire mental horror show, she found herself silently praying for the poor professor who was getting thoroughly raped in her own twisted fantasy.

-x-X-x-

[Later That Evening]

The entire Aleris estate was adorned in a manner so relentlessly magnificent that it made the ultra-rich guests genuinely question whether they were even worthy enough to step onto the property.

Every corner of the venue radiated a crushing sense of superiority that successfully reduced the proud aristocracy into stammering peasants who had suddenly forgotten the size of their own bank accounts.

Jax stood completely motionless beside Rudiger as his eyes scanned the grand banquet hall. The architecture was putting on an absolute masterclass in blinding people with wealth.

Gold-plated pillars and floating magical lanterns shimmered with such intense aggression that the room itself was somehow vastly more glittery than the noble ladies who had quite literally glued every available piece of makeup and diamond onto their skin just to stand out from the crowd.

Jax had deliberately kept himself attached to Rudiger’s side for the last few days as his servant was wearing the magical teleportation ring, and with Loki’s shadow still looming over everything, leaving him unguarded was simply not an option. If she decided to take Rudiger hostage, the consequences would be catastrophic.

While scanning the hall, his gaze landed on a familiar figure sitting in the far corner. The one person that most of the room’s eyes were also fixed on, though their stares carried an entirely different flavor. Judgmental whispers and disapproving glances were aimed at that corner like synchronized arrows.

And honestly, who could blame them? Because right there in the wine lounge, Roxana was making herself thoroughly comfortable with a bottle of wine in each hand while six or seven more stood loyally on her table like obedient soldiers waiting to be consumed.

Jax let out a long sigh and slapped his forehead at the painfully familiar sight before turning to Rudiger. "Give me a minute and stay right here. If you sense any danger, just scream my name."

He straightened up and started walking toward the wine lounge. "I am heading over to drag the biggest troublemaker in both our lives back to the mansion where she belongs. So do not follow me unless you either want to die on the spot or accidentally become the main character of this entire birthday party."

And with that, he made his way toward her table where Roxana was currently waging a deeply personal war against her newest bottle, struggling with both hands to lift it off the surface.

"What in the absolute flying fuck? I have only emptied three bottles so far and my body is already throwing a complete tantrum on me."

But then her eyes drifted down to the hand that was firmly pressing the bottle against the table, refusing to let it budge even a millimeter.

-x-X-x-
